testcases.input: words = ["one.two.three","four.five","six"], separator = "." Output: ["one","two","three","four","five","six"] Explanation: In this example we split as follows: "one.two.three" splits into "one", "two", "three" "four.five" splits into "four", "five" "six" splits into "six" Hence, the resulting array is ["one","two","three","four","five","six"].
testcases.expectedOutput: ["one","two","three","four","five","six"] Explanation: In this example we split as follows: "one.two.three" splits into "one", "two", "three" "four.five" splits into "four", "five" "six" splits into "six" Hence, the resulting array is ["one","two","three","four","five","six"].

testcases.input: words = ["$easy$","$problem$"], separator = "$" Output: ["easy","problem"] Explanation: In this example we split as follows: "$easy$" splits into "easy" (excluding empty strings) "$problem$" splits into "problem" (excluding empty strings) Hence, the resulting array is ["easy","problem"].
testcases.expectedOutput: ["easy","problem"] Explanation: In this example we split as follows: "$easy$" splits into "easy" (excluding empty strings) "$problem$" splits into "problem" (excluding empty strings) Hence, the resulting array is ["easy","problem"].

testcases.input: words = ["|||"], separator = "|" Output: [] Explanation: In this example the resulting split of "|||" will contain only empty strings, so we return an empty array [].
testcases.expectedOutput: [] Explanation: In this example the resulting split of "|||" will contain only empty strings, so we return an empty array [].
